---
title: "Tutorial"
format: html
toc: true
jupyter: python3
---

# OscarPredictions Tutorial

This tutorial turns the package workflow into a hands-on practice lab. You will install the package, initialize a workspace, generate features, run a model, and optionally test update/sync flows.

## Prerequisites

- Python 3.9+ installed.
- Internet access for package installation (and for optional update/sync scraping steps).
- A shell where you can run CLI commands.

If you are running this in Jupyter/Quarto, the cells below are executable as-is.

## 1) Set up helpers and a practice workspace

Run this first cell to define a helper for shell commands and create a local practice directory.

```{python}
from __future__ import annotations

import subprocess
from pathlib import Path


def run(cmd: str, cwd: Path | None = None) -> None:
    print(f"$ {cmd}")
    result = subprocess.run(
        cmd,
        cwd=str(cwd) if cwd else None,
        shell=True,
        text=True,
        capture_output=True,
    )
    if result.stdout:
        print(result.stdout)
    if result.stderr:
        print(result.stderr)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        raise RuntimeError(f"Command failed with exit code {result.returncode}: {cmd}")


workspace = Path("tutorial_workspace").resolve()
workspace.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
print(f"Workspace: {workspace}")
```

Checkpoint:
- Confirm the printed path exists on disk.

## 2) Install the package (TestPyPI)

This uses the same install command from the README.

```{python}
run(
    "python3 -m pip install "
    "--index-url https://test.pypi.org/simple/ "
    "--extra-index-url https://pypi.org/simple/ "
    "oscar-predictions"
)
```

Practice:
- Re-run the cell and observe that `pip` reports the package as already satisfied.

## 3) Verify the CLI is available

```{python}
run("oscar --help")
```

If `oscar` is not found in your environment, try:

```{python}
run("python3 -m oscar_predictions.cli --help")
```

Checkpoint:
- You should see command groups like `init-data`, `build-features`, `check-updates`, `model`, `reset`, and `sync`.

## 4) Initialize bundled base data

Initialize package data into the tutorial workspace.

```{python}
run(f"oscar init-data --workspace-dir {workspace}")
```

Check which files were created:

```{python}
for p in sorted(workspace.iterdir()):
    print(p.name)
```

Practice:
- Run `oscar init-data --workspace-dir <path> --overwrite` and compare behavior.

## 5) Build post-cleaning features

Generate derived feature tables from the base data.

```{python}
run(f"oscar build-features --workspace-dir {workspace}")
```

Inspect the expected feature outputs:

```{python}
expected = [
    "actor_year_award_matrix.csv",
    "film_actors_awards_sums_up_to_that_point.csv",
    "movies_with_cast_award_totals.csv",
]
for name in expected:
    path = workspace / name
    print(f"{name}: {'present' if path.exists() else 'missing'}")
```

Practice:
- Open `movies_with_cast_award_totals.csv` and identify at least three model features.

## 6) Run modeling

Train and evaluate the model using the tutorial workspace.

```{python}
report_path = workspace / "report.json"
preds_path = workspace / "predictions.csv"
run(
    f"oscar model --workspace-dir {workspace} "
    f"--report-json {report_path} --predictions-csv {preds_path}"
)
```

Confirm output artifacts:

```{python}
for out_path in [report_path, preds_path]:
    print(f"{out_path.name}: {'present' if out_path.exists() else 'missing'}")
```

Practice:
- Re-run modeling with `--seed 42 --test-size 0.25` and compare outputs.

## 7) Optional: Practice reset, update checks, and sync

These are optional because update/sync may take longer and require live scraping.

### Reset (safe local practice)

```{python}
run(f"oscar reset --workspace-dir {workspace} --dry-run")
```

Try a real reset to a different historical cutoff:

```{python}
run(f"oscar reset --workspace-dir {workspace} --cutoff-year 2021")
```

After resetting, rebuild features:

```{python}
run(f"oscar build-features --workspace-dir {workspace}")
```

### Check updates (networked scraping)

```{python}
# Optional: enable if you want to run update checks against IMDb
# run(f"oscar check-updates --workspace-dir {workspace}")
print("Uncomment the run(...) line above to execute check-updates.")
```

### End-to-end sync

Use a dry run first:

```{python}
run(f"oscar sync --workspace-dir {workspace} --dry-run")
```

Then optionally run a bounded sync:

```{python}
# Optional bounded sync example:
# run(
#     f"oscar sync --workspace-dir {workspace} --max-movies 5 --max-actors 200"
# )
print("Uncomment the run(...) block above to execute bounded sync.")
```

## Troubleshooting

- `oscar: command not found`: use `python3 -m oscar_predictions.cli --help` to verify install path, then reinstall in the active environment.
- Install fails on TestPyPI: retry with the exact index/extra-index flags shown above.
- Permissions/path issues: choose a writable workspace directory and rerun `init-data`.
- Slow or flaky update/sync runs: start with dry-run or capped scraping (`--max-movies`, `--max-actors`).

## Next steps

- Read [Documentation](documentation.md) for architecture and module-level details.
- Use [Technical Report](report.md) to connect model outputs to methodology and findings.
